name: Cronograma Creator API Catalog
description: Cronograma Creator exposes two API surfaces: (1) a single public HTTP endpoint to mint short share URLs, and (2) a browser-side JavaScript API available on window.CronogramaAPI when the app is loaded.

apis.title: Create publicly-shareable cronograma
apis.description: POST a cronograma state and receive a short URL. No auth. Rate-limited per IP.

400.error: string — invalid JSON or invalid state

apis.title: In-page JavaScript API
apis.description: Available on window.CronogramaAPI once the app is loaded. Use via Playwright/Puppeteer or directly from devtools.
loadVia: Open https://juangenovardortiz.github.io/cronograma_creator/ in a browser (or page.goto in Playwright/Puppeteer).
Optional: append ?mode=agent for deterministic, side-effect-free behavior.

methods.name: createFromJSON
methods.signature: createFromJSON(stateOrJSON: object | string) => { success: boolean, errors?: string[] }
methods.description: Loads a schedule from a state object or JSON string. Replaces what is currently on screen.

methods.name: setState
methods.description: Alias for createFromJSON when input is guaranteed to be an object.

methods.name: getState
methods.description: Returns the full state object for the cronograma currently rendered. Conforms to state.schema.json.

methods.name: getShareUrl
methods.description: Builds the legacy stateless share URL (#s=<base64>). For short URLs, use the HTTP endpoint above.

methods.name: getImageDataUrl
methods.description: Renders the cronograma to a PNG and returns a base64 data URL.

methods.name: validateState
methods.signature: validateState(stateObject: object) => { valid: boolean, errors: string[] }
methods.description: Returns true with empty errors if state is valid; otherwise lists problems.

methods.name: selfTest
methods.signature: selfTest() => Promise<{ success: boolean, summary: string, passed: object[], failed: object[] }>
methods.description: Runs an in-page self-test and returns a structured report.

urlParams.?c=<short-id>: Load a publicly-shared cronograma by its short id.
urlParams.#s=<base64>: Load a cronograma encoded directly in the hash (legacy).
urlParams.?mode=agent: Agent mode: no confirms, deterministic render, console logs prefixed [CronogramaAI].
urlParams.?autoexport=url|image|json: Auto-run the corresponding export on page load and write result JSON to #autoexport-result.

domHooks.#app-test-state: data-share-url, data-state (JSON), data-image-ready set after every render.
domHooks.#autoexport-result: Present after ?autoexport=. Contains { success, type, data } as JSON in dataset.result.

auth.note: All documented endpoints are public. The optional sign-in (Google via Supabase) only affects per-user cloud persistence inside the app and is never required to create, view, or share a cronograma.
